zh.lifeSummary: 當Herbert de St Quintin於1303年6月17日出生在英國越可爾布藍德斯波頓時，他的父親Herbert Saint Quintin III17歲，母親Lora de Fauconberge13歲。他於大約1339年與Margery de L'Isle在英國多瑟特福洛 聖 昆丁結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1347年7月10日在英國維爾爾斯坦頓 聖 昆丁享年44歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Southern French: variant of   St  .   Romain  .

variants.name: Romain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Alain, Altagrace, Aurore, Camille, Dominique, Edele, Georges, Henri, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Jean-Michel, Jeanmarie.
French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and English: variant of   Roman  .

nameMeanings.name: Quintin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Yves, Andre, Dominique, Gilles, Lucien, Michel, Oliva, Roch.
Breton: from the personal name Quintin, which is of the same Latin origin as 2 below, but also considered a derivative of Old Breton kint ‘first’.
French; Spanish (Quintín): from the personal name Quintin (Spanish Quintín), from Latin Quintinus, a Christian saint's name (see Quentin ).

variants.name: Quinton
meanings: English: from the Old French and Middle English personal name Quentin, Quintin (Latin Quintinus, a suffixed form of quintus ‘fifth’), popular in France from the cult of Saint Quentin of Amiens, and brought to England by the Normans. The surname was altered to Quinton by association with the placenames in 2 below.

English: habitational name from from any of three places called Quinton (Gloucestershire, Northamptonshire, Worcestershire) or from Quainton (Buckinghamshire). The placenames probably derive from Old English cwēn ‘queen’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, or perhaps from Old English cwene ‘woman’ + tūn or an Old English personal name Cwēna + Old English connective -ing- + tūn.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Saint-Quentin (Manche) in France or possibly from Saint-Quentin-en-Tourmont (Somme), the site of the martyrdom of Saint Quentin of Amiens, a 3rd-century Roman missionary to Gaul. It is not certain that this surname survived into the modern period.

variants.name: Quentin
meanings: French: from the Old French personal name Quentin (from Latin Quintinus, a suffixed form of quintus ‘fifth’), popular in France from the cult of Saint Quentin of Amiens, a 3rd-century Roman missionary to Gaul. Compare Quintin .

nameMeanings.name: Herbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. An Old English form, Herebeorht, existed in England before the Conquest, but was superseded by the Norman form, which gave rise to an important surname. The family in question were earls of Pembroke in the 16th and 17th centuries; they included the poet George Herbert . By the end of the Middle Ages Herbert was little used, although it remained a favourite with some families, notably the Saint Quintins of East Yorkshire. Its greater frequency in Britain from the 19th century onwards is due partly to the trend for the revival of medieval names of Germanic origin and partly to the trend for the transferred use of surnames.

variants.name: Heribert
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. An Old English form, Herebeorht, existed in England before the Conquest, but was superseded by the Norman form, which gave rise to an important surname. The family in question were earls of Pembroke in the 16th and 17th centuries; they included the poet George Herbert . By the end of the Middle Ages Herbert was little used, although it remained a favourite with some families, notably the Saint Quintins of East Yorkshire. Its greater frequency in Britain from the 19th century onwards is due partly to the trend for the revival of medieval names of Germanic origin and partly to the trend for the transferred use of surnames.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Herbert de St Quintin was born on 17 June 1303, in Brandesburton, Yorkshire, England, his father, Herbert Saint Quintin III, was 17 and his mother, Lora de Fauconberge, was 13. He married Margery de L'Isle about 1339, in Frome St Quintin, Dorset, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 10 July 1347, in Stanton St Quintin, Wiltshire, England, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Herbert de St Quintin est né le 17 juin 1303, à Brandesburton, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Herbert Saint Quintin III, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Lora de Fauconberge, avait 13 ans. Il a épousé Margery de L'Isle environ 1339, à Frome St Quintin, Dorset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 10 juillet 1347, à Stanton St Quintin, Wiltshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Herbert de St Quintin am 17. Juni 1303 in Brandesburton, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Herbert Saint Quintin III 17 und seine Mutter Lora de Fauconberge 13. Er hat um 1339 in Frome St Quintin, Dorset, England Margery de L'Isle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 10. Juli 1347 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Stanton St Quintin, Wiltshire, England.

Lui sposato Margery de L'Isle circa 1339, nel Frome St Quintin, Dorset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Herbert de St Quintinが1303年6月17日にイギリスヨークシュアイアブランデスブルトンで生まれた時，父Herbert Saint Quintin IIIは17歳，母Lora de Fauconbergeは13歳であった。約1339年にイギリスドールセトフロメ スト クインティンでMargery de L'Isleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1347年7月10日にイギリスウィルトシュアイアスタントン スト クインティンで44歳で死去した。

ko.Frome St. Quintin, Dorsetshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 프롬 스트 퀸틴
ko.lifeSummary: Herbert de St Quintin(이)가 1303년 6월 17일 영국 요르크실르 브랜데스버턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Herbert Saint Quintin III(은)는 17세였고, 그의 어머니 Lora de Fauconberge(은)는 13세였다. 그는 대략 1339년 영국 도르세트 프롬 스트 퀸틴에서 Margery de L'Isle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1347년 7월 10일 영국 윌츠히르 스탠턴 스트 퀸틴에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.Frome St. Quintin, Dorsetshire, England: Frome St Quintin, Dorset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Herbert de St Quintin nasceu em 17 de junho de 1303, em Brandesburton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Herbert Saint Quintin III, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Lora de Fauconberge, tinha 13 anos. Ele casou-se com Margery de L'Isle aproximadamente 1339, em Frome St Quintin, Dorset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 10 de julho de 1347, em Stanton St Quintin, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, com 44 anos.

ru.Frome St. Quintin, Dorsetshire, England: фром ст куинтинь, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Herbert de St Quintin родился 17 июня 1303 г. в местности брэндесбуртон, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Herbert Saint Quintin III было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Lora de Fauconberge, 13. Он женился на женщине по имени Margery de L'Isle около 1339 г. в местности фром ст куинтинь, дорсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 10 июля 1347 г. в местности стантон ст куинтинь, уилцхир, Англия в возрасте 44 лет.
